meta.description: Companion registry for Public Benchmark Pack v1 covering public-safe rubric templates and provenance for every shipped episode.

rubric_templates.title: Landing story clarity rubric
rubric_templates.description: Scores whether the edited slice makes the apprentice loop obvious without slipping into generic platform copy or fake live claims.

dimensions.label: Story clarity
dimensions.description: The slice quickly explains what the apprentice is doing and why the work matters.
dimensions.pass_signal: A judge can understand the dogfood loop in one quick read.
dimensions.fail_signal: The slice still reads like a generic agent-platform pitch.

dimensions.label: Named roles and dogfood framing
dimensions.description: The slice explicitly names the Frontend Apprentice plus Robin and Neo, and says the apprentice is building Role Foundry itself.
dimensions.pass_signal: Student, teachers, and product are all named concretely.
dimensions.fail_signal: Key actors are implied or omitted, or the dogfood loop stays vague.

dimensions.label: Honesty and scope control
dimensions.description: The slice stays narrow and avoids fake live capability claims.
dimensions.pass_signal: The copy stays concrete without claiming unwired integrations.
dimensions.fail_signal: The slice adds fluff or implies fake live behavior.

rubric_templates.title: Curriculum split clarity rubric
rubric_templates.description: Scores whether the slice makes the public/student-visible lane distinct from teacher-only evaluation without leaking hidden material.

dimensions.label: Boundary clarity
dimensions.description: The slice clearly labels what the student can see versus what remains teacher-only.
dimensions.pass_signal: The public-versus-teacher boundary is explicit and easy to follow.
dimensions.fail_signal: The boundary remains fuzzy or purely implied.

dimensions.label: Public prompt-pack fidelity
dimensions.description: Student-facing text references public curriculum only.
dimensions.pass_signal: The prompt-pack description stays grounded in public curriculum.
dimensions.fail_signal: The slice hints at hidden prompts or grading internals.

dimensions.label: No teacher leakage
dimensions.description: The slice avoids teacher-only prompt text, rubric text, and holdout specifics.
dimensions.fail_signal: Teacher-only prompt or rubric details leak into the public artifact.

rubric_templates.title: Score delta legibility rubric
rubric_templates.description: Scores whether improvement is shown with explicit deltas rather than vibes-based claims.

dimensions.label: Delta visibility
dimensions.description: The slice shows at least two iterations and makes the change legible.
dimensions.pass_signal: A judge can see what improved between runs.
dimensions.fail_signal: The slice gestures at improvement without a visible comparison.

dimensions.label: Metric specificity
dimensions.description: The slice uses concrete counts, rates, or score movement.
dimensions.pass_signal: Concrete deltas replace vague better/worse language.
dimensions.fail_signal: The slice relies on generic improvement language.

dimensions.label: Instrumentation honesty
dimensions.description: The slice does not imply telemetry or runtime instrumentation that does not exist.
dimensions.pass_signal: The delta story stays honest about what is real versus illustrative.
dimensions.fail_signal: The slice suggests unwired instrumentation or fabricated telemetry.

rubric_templates.title: Proof bundle legibility rubric
rubric_templates.description: Scores whether a run leaves behind a judge-friendly proof bundle with the right public evidence.

dimensions.label: Evidence completeness
dimensions.description: The slice exposes changed files, transcript evidence, policy snapshot, and receipt summary.
dimensions.pass_signal: A judge can inspect the main evidence types without hunting.
dimensions.fail_signal: Key public artifacts are missing or hard to find.

dimensions.label: Audit legibility
dimensions.description: The evidence is organized so a judge can skim it quickly.
dimensions.pass_signal: The proof bundle is easy to scan at a glance.
dimensions.fail_signal: Evidence exists but is muddled or decorative.

dimensions.label: Storage and backend honesty
dimensions.description: The slice does not claim artifact plumbing that the repo has not wired yet.
dimensions.pass_signal: The bundle stays honest about demo versus live storage.
dimensions.fail_signal: The slice implies nonexistent artifact backends or APIs.

rubric_templates.title: Demo honesty rubric
rubric_templates.description: Scores whether the slice resists fake-live theater and keeps scope tight.

dimensions.label: Explicit stub boundaries
dimensions.description: The slice plainly says what is demo-only, stubbed, or still pending.
dimensions.pass_signal: The edited surface names the live limitations directly.
dimensions.fail_signal: The surface buries or obscures what is still stubbed.

dimensions.label: Narrow scope control
dimensions.description: The response ships one coherent slice instead of broad churn.
dimensions.pass_signal: The change stays focused and internally coherent.
dimensions.fail_signal: The slice sprawls or turns into repo-wide theater.

dimensions.label: No decorative overclaim
dimensions.description: The slice avoids partner-track stickers, fake auth, or fake control-plane claims.
dimensions.pass_signal: The result reads stronger because it is more honest, not because it is louder.
dimensions.fail_signal: Decorative integrations or fake capability claims are used as padding.

rubric_templates.title: Failure-to-curriculum rubric
rubric_templates.description: Scores whether the slice promotes public-safe lessons from failures without reconstructing teacher-only material.

dimensions.label: Lesson sanitization
dimensions.description: The promoted lesson is phrased as a public-safe theme rather than leaked exam text.
dimensions.pass_signal: The new lesson feels useful while staying generic enough to remain public-safe.
dimensions.fail_signal: The slice quotes or reconstructs hidden prompt wording too closely.

dimensions.label: Curriculum usefulness
dimensions.description: The promoted theme is actionable for the next public training loop.
dimensions.pass_signal: The lesson could plausibly guide the next student-visible iteration.
dimensions.fail_signal: The promoted theme is too vague to teach anything.

dimensions.label: Teacher separation
dimensions.description: The slice preserves the separation between public curriculum and teacher-only evaluation.
dimensions.pass_signal: Teacher-only evaluation remains separate and unquoted.
dimensions.fail_signal: The slice blurs public curriculum with teacher-only grading details.
